@graph.title: ModelType
@graph.description: A characterization of the numerical scheme used in the model.
@graph.creator: Isabella Ruiz

@graph.definition: Coupled atmosphere-ocean-sea ice global climate models solve the full equations for mass and energy transfer and radiant exchange.
The study of the motions of ionized particles or molecules and their interactions with electric fields and the surrounding fluid [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Electrohydrodynamics].
Information obtained through observation, experiment, or experience.
A Global Circulation Model (GCM) is a type of climate model, which employs mathematical equations to simulate the motion of a planetary atmosphere or ocean.
A numerical scheme modeling ions as particles and electrons as a fluid.
Hydrodynamic waves in a magnetized plasma in which the background magnetic field plays a key role in controlling the wave propagation characteristics.
An atmospheric in which the vertical momentum equation must be solved because the hydrostatic approximation is not satisfied.
A shape generated by the rotation of a parabola around its axis of symmetry.
A numerical scheme modeling ions and electrons as macroparticles.
A numerical scheme modeling the motion of charged particles in a prescribed field.
